message:
feat(engine): rewrite semantic twin state-space tests for lane_decision_default API and fix adjustment strategy assertion

Rewrites the semantic_twin_state_space integration tests to target
the new SemanticTwinSpecification::lane_decision_default() constructor
instead of the previous frx_19_1_default() API. The old tests validated
the full falsification runtime flow via SemanticTwinRuntime; the new
tests validate the specification-level contracts directly:

- lane_decision_default_exposes_expected_contracts: verifies component
  identity (SEMANTIC_TWIN_COMPONENT), treatment/outcome variable
  bindings (lane_choice → latency_outcome), and presence of required
  assumptions (nondeterminism_log_complete) and transitions
  (fallback_to_safe_mode)
- causal_model_backdoor_recommendation_is_consistent: confirms the
  causal model's backdoor criterion produces an identified adjustment
  set matching the spec's recommended_adjustment_set
- assumption_ledger_from_spec_is_deterministic_and_actionable: builds
  two ledgers from the same spec and asserts identical assumption
  counts, monitor counts, and chain hashes; then violates a monitor
  and asserts exactly one action with chain hash divergence
- snapshot_validation_requires_mandatory_fields: ensures partial
  snapshots missing required measurement contracts fail validation

Also fixes the adjustment strategy assertion in semantic_twin.rs to
accept effects with an empty adjustment set when they have no blocked
confounding paths (previously required non-empty adjustment set
unconditionally, which was overly strict for unconfounded effects).

Includes rustfmt formatting applied to method chains and match arms.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
